title: Carnivorous seaweed
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Seaweed.jpg]] |name=Carnivorous seaweed |designation=At least [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]]''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'' |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=Thorned [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] |lifespan= |origin=[[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] |habitat=[[Water/Legends|Aquatic]] |diet=[[Diet/Legends|Carnivorous]] |language= }} {{Quote|Weird.''"
"''Interesting… Uh, then again… maybe 'weird' is a better word for it.|Jaina and Jacen Solo, on carnivorous seaweed|}} '''Carnivorous seaweed''' was an [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Plant/Legends|plant]] that grew in the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] and used its [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] to [[Death/Legends|suffocate]] its victims. In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainees]] [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]], [[Lowbacca]], [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] were nearly killed by a patch of carnivorous [[Seaweed/Legends|seaweed]] in the [[Hapan]] ocean when [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Yfra]] [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]] their [[Hapan Water Dragon|wavespeeder]]. Later, the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] drove their wavespeeder into some carnivorous seaweed to kill a group of [[Bartokk]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] who were following them. Those assassins were sent by Yfra to kill the [[Matriarch/Legends|matriarch]] of Hapes, [[Ta'a Chume]], so Yfra could assume the role of Hapan [[Queen/Legends|monarch]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|I can't find its brain. It seems all reflexes. All I can sense is that it's hungry, hungry.|Jacen Solo, on carnivorous seaweed|}} [[Diet/Legends|Carnivorous]] [[Seaweed/Legends|seaweed]] was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Plant/Legends|plant]] that grew in at least [[Unidentified Hapan ocean|one]] of the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]]. The seaweed had many [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]], which were [[Color/Legends|green]], thick, and rubbery with long thorns. When cut, the tentacles oozed a syrupy green liquid. The carnivorous seaweed grabbed prey with its tentacles and then [[Death/Legends|suffocated]] it by constriction.''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' The plant also grew large scarlet [[Flower/Legends|flowers]], with blue [[Fruit/Legends|fruits]] in the center that resembled [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], who was [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] at [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum]], tried and failed to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to find the [[Brain/Legends|brain]] of a patch of carnivorous seaweed. [[Gender/Legends|He]] stated that the seaweed seemed to be all reflexes and hunger but that it was also at least partially [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], as it could respond to its surroundings. ==History== {{Quote|I'm not letting some weed get the best of me!|Jacen Solo, while fighting carnivorous seaweed|}} In 23 [[Great ReSynchronization|ABY]], a patch of carnivorous seaweed in the [[Hapan]] ocean nearly took the lives of [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainees]] [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]], [[Lowbacca]], and Jacen and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] after their [[Hapan Water Dragon|wavespeeder]] was [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]] by [[Yfra]], the Hapan [[Matriarch/Legends|matriarch's]] personal [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]], during her plot to overthrow the Hapan [[Queen/Legends|monarchy]]. After escaping from the seaweed by using their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and [[Knife/Legends|knives]], and returning to [[Reef Fortress]], where they were staying, the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] helped the matriarch, [[Ta'a Chume]], escape from [[Bartokk]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] by climbing out the [[Window/Legends|window]] of her [[Sleeper (furniture)|bedchamber]], in which she was trapped. Yfra had sent the assassins to kill the matriarch so Yfra herself could be the monarch in place of those of the [[Hapan Royal House|Hapan Royal Family]]. The Jedi trainees escaped the [[Reef Fortress Island|island]] in a small wavespeeder; however, they were chased by the Bartokks in their [[Assault boat/Legends|assault boat]]. The friends decided to steer their wavespeeder into some carnivorous seaweed in the hope that the assassins would follow them and be killed by the seaweed. The assassins did follow the wavespeeder, and, after Chume set their craft off-balance using a small [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] she was carrying, they were pulled into the ocean by the carnivorous seaweed. The young Jedi escaped the seaweed and delivered Chume to [[Fountain Palace|her palace]] in [[Time/Legends|time]] to stop Yfra from overthrowing the monarchy. {{Flora-stub}} ==Behind the scenes== Carnivorous seaweed first appeared in the [[1996]] young reader's book ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'', by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Rebecca Moesta]]. In [[1998]], it was mentioned in both [[Stephen J. Sansweet]]'s ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', under the entry for "[[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]," and [[Daniel Wallace]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'', the latter of which provided the seaweed's first illustration. It was also mentioned in [[James Luceno]]'s [[2000]] novel [[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse|''Jedi Eclipse'']] of the [[Agents of Chaos Duology|''Agents of Chaos'' Duology]]. Later, in [[2008]], it was mentioned in the entry for "Reef Fortress" in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'' {{1st}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' {{1stp}} *{{CSWECite|III||Reef Fortress}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Aquatic plants]] [[Category:Carnivorous plants]] [[Category:Plants of Hapes]] [[Category:Species of undefined sentience]]
